channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Oil Execs Predict Future WTI Oil Prices
item.description: Executives from oil and gas firms revealed where they expect the West Texas Intermediate crude oil price to be at various points in the future in the second quarter Dallas Fed Energy Survey.

item.title: Woodside assumes operatorship of Gippsland Basin assets
item.description: Woodside Energy has taken over as operator of the Gippsland Basin assets in southeastern Australia from Esso Australia Resources, including the GBJV and KUJV.

item.title: LNG Trade Could Match 2025 despite Mideast Crisis, Shell Says
item.description: 'The ramp up of new liquefaction facilities in North America, improved performance at existing plants and slower Asian imports of LNG have partially offset the impact of reduced supply from the Middle East'.

item.title: Eni, Mercuria Agree to Join Forces in Commodity Trading
item.description: Italian oil company Eni SpA and commodity merchant Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. signed an agreement to join forces in trading.

item.title: BP Sees More Leadership Changes
item.description: BP PLC announced the retirement of newly appointed deputy chief executive Carol Howle and named 2 new executive vice presidents from within its ranks.

item.title: Trump Tells Gasoline Retailers to Cut Prices Immediately
item.description: In a statement posted on his Truth Social page on Tuesday, U.S. President Donald J. Trump ordered U.S. gasoline retailers to cut their prices 'immediately'.

item.title: Europe’s Nuclear Ambitions Drive New Interest in Italian Uranium Exploration
item.description: A firm planning to mine uranium in northern Italy has drawn up plans for a London float as it seeks to capitalise on renewed interest in nuclear technology. Reveille Resources, which will be operated by Ippolito Ingo…

item.title: Renewables Hit Record 58% Share of Germany's Power Consumption
item.description: Renewable energy accounted for a record-high 58% of Germany’s electricity consumption in the first half of 2026, up from 55.8% for the same period last year, the latest estimates by industry associations ZSW and BDEW…

item.title: Petronas reports two new hydrocarbon finds in Suriname’s Block 52
item.description: Petronas, via its fully owned subsidiary Petronas Suriname, has reported two new hydrocarbon discoveries and a successful appraisal well in offshore Block 52, Suriname.

item.title: Transocean secures $1bn Equinor contract for three Norwegian rigs
item.description: Transocean has announced a new agreement with Equinor valued at over $1bn covering the deployment of three harsh environment semisubmersible rigs on the Norwegian Continental Shelf (NCS).

item.title: EIA: U.S. Crude Inventories Post Another Major Draw
item.description: Crude oil inventories in the United States decreased by 3.8 million barrels during the week ending June 26, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The decrease…

item.title: Oil's Softening Trend Continues
item.description: Standard Chartered Bank Energy Research Head Emily Ashford noted that oil's 'softening trend' had continued 'as the return of easy barrels outpaces demand recovery'.

item.title: Japan's Biggest LNG Buyer Creates Standalone Trading Arm
item.description: Japan’s JERA is creating a wholly-owned subsidiary to develop and manage its LNG, upstream, low-carbon fuels, and shipping businesses, the biggest Japanese LNG importer and largest power producer said on Wednesday. The…

item.title: ADNOC, Eni Acquire Stakes in Argentina LNG Blocks
item.description: The Vaca Muerta upstream assets are planned to supply feed gas to 2 floating liquefaction facilities with a combined capacity of 12 MMtpa.

item.title: U.S. Raises Pressure on Iran With Renewed Military Warning
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance has again signaled that the White House is prepared to use force against Iran if diplomacy fails, raising the stakes around a 60-day memorandum of understanding (MOU) that has halted open…

item.title: EU Industry Splits Over Emissions Trading Scheme Overhaul
item.description: European companies are concerned – in various ways – about the upcoming revamping of the EU's emissions trading scheme (ETS), the key instrument the bloc has been implementing since 2005 to curb emissions from…

item.title: Shell to divest Na Kika and Coulomb assets for $1.7bn
item.description: Shell through its subsidiary Shell Offshore is set to divest its 50% non-operated working stake in the Na Kika platform and associated fields located in the Gulf of America for $1.7bn.

item.title: US Makes Progress in Indirect Talks on Iran Deal
item.description: U.S. negotiators Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff had positive discussions with regional leaders in Qatar and technical talks with Iran are moving ahead, according to a senior administration official.

item.title: Eni Forms Trading Venture With Mercuria to Boost Commodity Profits
item.description: Eni is forming an energy commodity joint venture with global trader Mercuria as the Italian energy major eyes higher profits to rival the most active traders among the European energy giants. Eni on Wednesday said it…

item.title: KKR and SK Launch South Korea’s Largest Renewable Energy Platform
item.description: Global investment firm KKR and South Korea’s industrial conglomerate SK Inc are launching the single biggest renewable energy platform in South Korea to help meet growing power demand from AI and chip manufacturing. KKR…
